cacert_path.description: Use a CA certificate when performing server verification by providing the path to a CA certificate file.
cacert_path.title: CA Cert Path

cache_from.description: List of image names to use as cache source.
cache_from.title: Cache From

capabilities.description: List of capabilities to add to the container.
capabilities.title: Capabilities

cert_path.description: Path to the client's TLS certificate file used to authenticate with the Docker daemon.
cert_path.title: Cert Path

cgroupns_mode.description: Specify the cgroup namespace mode for the container.
cgroupns_mode.title: Cgroup Namespace Mode

command_handling.description: How the command and entrypoint options are handled when provided as a list.
command_handling.title: Command Handling

container_default_behavior.description: Various module options used to have default values in older versions of the module. This option controls what happens when the user does not specify a value for one of these options.
container_default_behavior.title: Container Default Behavior

devices.description: List of device mappings to add a host device to the container. The format is <device-on-host>[:<device-on-container>][:<permissions>].
devices.title: Devices

dns_servers.description: List of DNS servers for the container to use.
dns_servers.title: DNS Servers

docker_host.description: The URL or Unix socket path used to connect to the Docker API. Defaults to the value of the DOCKER_HOST environment variable or unix:///var/run/docker.sock.
docker_host.title: Docker Host

docker_networks.description: List of Docker networks to create. Each entry is passed to the docker_network module.
docker_networks.title: Docker Networks

dockerfile.description: Path to a Dockerfile.j2 file used to build the image.
dockerfile.title: Dockerfile

env.description: Set environment variables. This option allows you to specify arbitrary environment variables that are available for the process that will be launched inside of the container.
env.title: Environment

etc_hosts.description: Dict of host-to-IP mappings, where each host name is a key in the dictionary. Each host name will be added to the container's ``/etc/hosts`` file.
etc_hosts.title: etc hosts

exposed_ports.description: List of ports to expose on the container.
exposed_ports.title: Exposed Ports

force.description: Whether to always rebuild the image, even if it already exists.
force.title: Force Build

force_kill.description: Use the kill signal when removing the container.
force_kill.title: Force Kill

groups.description: List of inventory groups to add the instance to.
groups.title: Groups

hostname.description: Container host name. Sets the container host name that is available inside the container.
hostname.title: Hostname

image.description: Repository path (or image name) and tag used to create the container. If an image is not found, the image will be pulled from the registry. If no tag is included, latest will be used.
image.title: Image

keep_volumes.description: Whether to retain anonymous volumes associated with the container when it is removed.
keep_volumes.title: Keep Volumes

key_path.description: Path to the client's TLS key file used to authenticate with the Docker daemon.
key_path.title: Key Path

kill_signal.description: Signal sent to the container when killing it.
kill_signal.title: Kill Signal

labels.description: Additional labels to add to the container. The ``owner=molecule`` label is always added.
labels.title: Labels

links.description: List of container links to add to the container, in the form ``<name>:<alias>``.
links.title: Links

memory_swap.description: Total memory limit of the container (memory plus swap).
memory_swap.title: Memory Swap

mounts.description: List of mount dictionaries to attach to the container. Each entry is passed to the docker_container module's mounts option.
mounts.title: Mounts

network_mode.description: Set the network mode for the container. Choices are ``bridge``, ``host``, ``none``, ``container:<name|id>``, ``<network_name>`` or ``default``.
network_mode.title: Network Mode

networks.description: List of networks the container will be connected to.

aliases.description: Network aliases for the container on the given network.

ipv4_address.description: IPv4 address for the container on the given network.

name.description: Name of the network to connect to.
name.title: Name

networks.title: Networks

networks_cli_compatible.description: Whether the networks option is handled in a way compatible with the Docker CLI.
networks_cli_compatible.title: Networks CLI Compatible

override_command.description: Whether or not to override the default command set by the container image.
override_command.title: Override Command

pid_mode.description: Set the PID mode for the container.
pid_mode.title: PID Mode

platform.description: Platform to build the image for, in the format ``os[/arch[/variant]]``.
platform.title: Platform

pre_build_image.description: Use a pre-built image instead of building one from a Dockerfile.
pre_build_image.title: Pre Build Image

privileged.description: Give extended privileges to the container.
privileged.title: Privileged

published_ports.description: Publish a container's port, or range of ports, to the host. Format - "ip:hostPort:containerPort" | "ip::containerPort" | "hostPort:containerPort" | "containerPort".
published_ports.title: Published Ports

pull.description: Whether or not to pull the image.
pull.title: Pull

purge_networks.description: Whether to purge the container from networks not listed in networks after creating or recreating it.
purge_networks.title: Purge Networks

restart_policy.description: Restart policy to follow when containers exit. Valid values are * no - Do not restart containers on exit * on-failure - Restart containers when they exit with a non-0 exit code, retrying indefinitely or until the optional restart_retries count is hit * always - Restart containers when they exit, regardless of status, retrying indefinitely * unless-stopped - Restart containers when they exit, regardless of status, retrying indefinitely, except when the container is put into a stopped state manually.
restart_policy.title: Restart Policy

restart_retries.description: The number of times to retry restarting a container when the restart_policy is set to on-failure. This option is ignored when restart_policy is set to no or always.
restart_retries.title: Restart Retries

runtime.description: OCI runtime to use for the container.
runtime.title: Runtime

stop_signal.description: Signal sent to the container when stopping it.
stop_signal.title: Stop Signal

sysctls.description: Dictionary of sysctl options to set in the container.
sysctls.title: Sysctls

tls_verify.description: Require HTTPS and verify certificates when contacting the Docker daemon. If explicitly set to true, then TLS verification will be used. If set to false, then TLS verification will not be used.
tls_verify.title: TLS Verify

tmpfs.description: Mount a tmpfs directory in the container.
tmpfs.title: tmpfs

user.description: User to run the container as.
user.title: User

volumes.description: Create a bind mount. If you specify a volume ``/HOST-DIR:/CONTAINER-DIR``, Docker bind mounts ``/HOST-DIR`` on the host to ``/CONTAINER-DIR`` in the Docker container.
volumes.title: Volumes
